name: Roy Acuff
description: American country music singer and fiddler (1903-1992)
abstract: Roy Claxton Acuff (September 15, 1903 – November 23, 1992) was an American country music singer, fiddler, and promoter. Known as the "King of Country Music", Acuff is often credited with moving the genre from its early string band and "hoedown" format to the singer-based format that helped make it internationally successful.

concerts.label: Wild Turkey Festival of Country Music

about_quotes.speaker: John "Knocky" Parker
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, we liked them personally. Roy Acuff for a while was there in Fort Worth when he wrote Walking the Floor... we liked him okay, but he's kind of just a personality singer.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: Roy Acuff’s hit records from the mid-30s from Knoxville with Knoxville people were the ones that had a string bass in it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jeff Riddick
about_quotes.evidence: With all the guys like Roy, what's your name, on piano, and Shorty Reese on tenor, and Frank Federico, and all of them.

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: And it’s true, you listen to Roy Acuff or Kitty Wells or the best of them all I think, Merle Haggard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: Nobody performed there but Country-and- Western. Roy Acuff. Hank Williams.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
